witnesses.description: An approximate solution whose exact residual infinity norm is within the claimed bound.
An exact rational solution contained in a coarse interval box.
A diagonally dominant 2x2 system with one exact Jacobi step and a contraction check.

checks.claim: The listed approximate solution has residual infinity norm at most the claimed exact bound.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes A*x_hat - b and the infinity norm exactly over rational numbers.

checks.claim: The exact solution of the fixed 2x2 system lies inside the listed rational interval box.
checks.notes: The validator checks A*x = b exactly and then checks each component against the listed lower and upper bounds.

checks.claim: The first Jacobi update for the fixed system reduces the infinity-norm error under the listed contraction bound.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the Jacobi step, exact solution, error norms, row-sum contraction bound, and error inequality.

checks.claim: The same approximate solution cannot have residual infinity norm at most 1/2.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the exact residual norm as 1, so the claimed 1/2 bound is rejected; the Axeyum regression refutes residual_inf_norm = 1 together with residual_inf_norm <= 1/2 using rechecked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The first Jacobi update for the fixed system has infinity-norm error at most 1/8.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the first Jacobi step and exact solution, then recomputes ||x1 - x*||_inf = 7/44; the Axeyum regression refutes that exact value together with the false claimed bound 1/8 using rechecked Farkas evidence.
